title: What to know about Corydalis yanhusuo before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Corydalis yanhusuo are the relief of arthritis symptoms through its anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to the presence of tetrahydropalmatine and protopine, which are responsible for inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Additionally, Corydalis yanhusuo has been used to alleviate anxiety by modulating the activity of GABA and serotonin receptors, facilitating a reduction in anxiety-related behaviors. The plant's analgesic and anti-inflammatory constituents, including tetrahydropalmatine and protopine, also contribute to its effectiveness in treating chronic pain and stroke-induced pain. Furthermore, Corydalis yanhusuo's antidepressant properties are thought to be due to the presence of tetrahydropalmatine, which has been shown to increase the expression of brain-derived neurotrophic factor. Its anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties also make it a potential treatment for conditions such as epilepsy, inflammation, migraine, and neuropathy.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Corydalis yanhusuo are multifaceted, demonstrating analgesic effects by alleviating pain in various conditions. Its anti-inflammatory properties contribute to reducing swelling and inflammation, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids such as dehydrocorybulbine. Additionally, the plant exhibits antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals and protecting cells from oxidative damage, while also showcasing antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of microorganisms. Furthermore, Corydalis yanhusuo has been found to possess antispasmodic and anticonvulsant properties, reducing muscle spasms and seizure activity, respectively. The plant's neuroprotective effects, coupled with its anxiolytic and nociceptive properties, enable it to provide pain relief and modulate pain perception, making it a valuable asset in traditional medicine.
identification: Corydalis yanhusuo is a perennial plant native to China and the Himalayas, belonging to the Papaveraceae family. It grows up to 60 cm in height, with ovate-shaped leaves that are 2-5 cm long and 1-3 cm wide, arranged oppositely on the stem. The plant's inflorescence is a terminal raceme bearing pale yellow to white flowers, 2-3 cm in diameter, with four petals and a distinctive spur. Corydalis yanhusuo is typically found in mountainous regions, often growing in rocky areas and along streams, at elevations ranging from 1,000 to 3,500 meters above sea level. The plant's geographical distribution is most extensive in China, with scattered populations in the Himalayas and other parts of Asia.
cultivation: Corydalis yanhusuo is a hardy perennial that thrives in temperate climates with growth habits characterized by slow establishment and subsequent rapid spreading through underground rhizomes and stolons. It requires full sun to partial shade and well-drained soils with a pH range of 6.0-7.0, tolerating a wide range of temperatures from -10 to 30°C. To promote optimal growth, it is essential to maintain consistent moisture, avoid over-fertilization, and provide support for the stems as they can become top-heavy with flowering spikes. Regular pruning and division can help control the plant's spread and encourage new growth, while mulching around the base can help retain moisture and suppress weeds. By providing the right conditions and following these growth tips, Corydalis yanhusuo can be successfully cultivated as a low-maintenance groundcover or medicinal herb.
history: Corydalis yanhusuo is a traditional Chinese medicinal plant with a long history of use dating back to the Tang Dynasty (618-907 AD). It has been employed to treat a variety of conditions, including rheumatism, fever, and digestive issues, as well as to promote blood circulation and relieve pain. The plant's alkaloids, particularly tetrahydropalmatine and protopine, have been found to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Corydalis yanhusuo is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its efficacy, and its use is commonly seen in the treatment of conditions such as arthritis and sprains. Clinical studies have demonstrated the plant's potential as a treatment for pain and inflammation, with some research suggesting its efficacy in reducing symptoms of conditions such as osteoarthritis.

constituents.name: Corydaline
constituents.description: Corydaline is a pyridone alkaloid with analgesic and sedative properties.

constituents.name: Berberine
constituents.description: Berberine is a yellow, crystalline alkaloid with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids are a type of compound found in Corydalis yanhusuo that have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Corydalis yanhusuo alkaloids have been found to exhibit analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids are present in Corydalis yanhusuo, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular protective properties.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Saponins are a type of bioactive compound found in Corydalis yanhusuo, characterized by their surface-active properties and ability to form stable foam in aqueous solutions.

constituents.name: Corynoline
constituents.description: Corynoline is a benzylisoquinoline alkaloid with analgesic and sedative properties.

constituents.name: Matrine
constituents.description: An alkaloid compound isolated from Corydalis yanhusuo, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and cardiovascular stimulant properties

constituents.name: tetrahydroprotoberberine
constituents.description: Tetrahydroprotoberberine is a tetrahydroisoquinoline alkaloid with analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties.

constituents.name: Protoberberine
constituents.description: Protoberberine is an isoquinoline alkaloid with anti-inflammatory, anti-bacterial, and anti-viral properties.

constituents.name: Tetrahydroprotoberberine
constituents.description: A tetrahydroprotoberberine alkaloid found in Corydalis yanhusuo, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, with potential cardiovascular and neuroprotective effects.

constituents.name: Protopine
constituents.description: Protopine is an alkaloid constituent of the plant Corydalis yanhusuo with analgesic and spasmolytic properties.

constituents.name: Yanhusuo alkaloids
constituents.description: Yanhusuo alkaloids are a group of compounds with analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties.

constituents.name: Tetrahydropalmatine
constituents.description: Tetrahydropalmatine is an alkaloid constituent of Corydalis yanhusuo that exhibits analgesic, antispasmodic, and sedative properties.

constituents.name: Corydalisine
constituents.description: Corydalisine is an alkaloid constituent of Corydalis yanhusuo, which is a bisbenzylisoquinoline with analgesic and spasmolytic properties.

constituents.name: Yanhusuine
constituents.description: Yanhusuine is a medicinal constituent of Corydalis yanhusuo that exhibits analgesic and sedative properties.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: Corydalis yanhusuo terpenoids exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Sanguinarine
constituents.description: Sanguinarine is a quinazolinic alkaloid with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor properties.

constituents.name: Palmatine
constituents.description: Palmatine is a quinazoline alkaloid with anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and vasodilatory properties.

constituents.name: Yanhuangine
constituents.description: Yanhuangine is a medicinal constituent of Corydalis yanhusuo with analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Nausea occurs due to the stimulation of the brain's chemoreceptor trigger zone by tetrahydroprotoberberine alkaloids present in Corydalis yanhusuo.
Dizziness occurs due to decreased blood pressure and decreased vasopressin levels caused by the anticholinergic and antihistamine properties of Corydalis yanhusuo.
Corydalis yanhusuo extracts cause abdominal pain in some individuals
Corydalis yanhusuo may cause gastrointestinal irritation resulting in loose stools
Corydalis yanhusuo can cause headache due to its active ingredients, including tetrahydropalmatine, which is a known vasoconstrictor.
Subjective feeling of physical or mental exhaustion reported by some individuals taking Corydalis yanhusuo.
Corydalis yanhusuo overdose leads to suppression of respiratory drive and decreased breathing rate.
Corydalis yanhusuo causes a decrease in blood pressure.
Seizures occur in individuals taking Corydalis yanhusuo due to its neurotoxic alkaloid content, particularly tetrahydroprotoberberine.
Corydalis yanhusuo contains tetrahydropalmatine, which has been shown to cause liver damage in animal studies.

uses_list.description: This plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which make it useful in treating the pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
The plant's flavonoids and alkaloids have been shown to have a sedative effect, which can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Research has found that the plant's flavonoids and alkaloids have neuroprotective properties, which can help to reduce the damage caused by a stroke and improve recovery outcomes.
The plant's flavonoids and alkaloids have been shown to have a mood-elevating effect, which can help to reduce symptoms of depression.
The plant's flavonoids and alkaloids have been found to have anticonvulsant properties, which can help to reduce the frequency and severity of seizures associated with epilepsy.
The plant has been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating chronic pain conditions.
The plant's flavonoids and alkaloids have been shown to have a vasoconstrictive effect, which can help to reduce the blood flow to the brain and alleviate migraine symptoms.
The plant has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in conditions such as sprains, strains, and other injuries.
The plant has analgesic properties, which can help to reduce pain associated with various conditions, such as headaches, back pain, and fibromyalgia.
The plant's flavonoids and alkaloids have been found to have neuroprotective properties, which can help to reduce the damage caused by neuropathy and improve symptoms.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Corydalis yanhusuo is used to treat chronic pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are pro-inflammatory mediators. Depression, often characterized by reduced dopamine levels, is also alleviated by the plant's ability to increase dopamine release, thereby improving mood. Additionally, Corydalis yanhusuo has anxiolytic properties, reducing anxiety by modulating the activity of the GABA receptor, a key inhibitory neurotransmitter. The plant is also used in the treatment of Parkinson's disease, multiple sclerosis, inflammation, arthritis, neuropathy, epilepsy, stroke, and migraines, often by modulating the activity of various neurotransmitters and ion channels. Furthermore, it has been used to alleviate symptoms of fibromyalgia, insomnia, muscle spasms, migraines, diabetes, osteoporosis, stress, rheumatoid arthritis, and gout, often by inhibiting pro-inflammatory pathways and promoting bone health.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Corydalis yanhusuo is used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, chronic pain, and inflammation, which are often managed through the plant's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The plant's sedative effects also make it an effective treatment for anxiety, migraines, and insomnia, allowing patients to experience improved sleep quality and reduced stress levels. Furthermore, Corydalis yanhusuo has been shown to have neuroprotective properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of depression, stroke, and neuropathy, as well as potentially reducing the risk of hypertension and diabetes. Additionally, the plant's anti-cancer properties have been studied in relation to cancer treatment, and its ability to reduce muscle spasms and fever makes it a valuable resource in traditional chinese medicine. Corydalis yanhusuo has also been researched for its potential in treating epilepsy, multiple sclerosis, rheumatoid arthritis, nausea, fatigue, and rheumatism, making it a versatile and widely used medicinal plant.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Corydalis yanhusuo is used to alleviate pain, which is often associated with arthritis, as well as to treat stroke by improving blood flow. Its anti-inflammatory properties also make it effective in treating neuropathy and fibromyalgia, conditions characterized by chronic pain and inflammation. Moreover, Corydalis yanhusuo has been shown to have anxiolytic and antiepileptic effects, which can help manage anxiety and epilepsy. Additionally, it may help regulate blood sugar levels, making it a potential treatment for diabetes, and has been studied for its potential to prevent or slow the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Furthermore, Corydalis yanhusuo has been found to have neuroprotective properties, which can help mitigate the effects of multiple sclerosis, Parkinson's disease, and hypertension, as well as alleviate symptoms of migraines, depression, cancer, rheumatism, rheumatoid arthritis, trigeminal neuralgia, muscle spasm, and inflammation.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Corydalis yanhusuo is used to treat rheumatoid arthritis, which involves inflammation and pain in the joints, as well as nerve pain and sciatica, by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles. It is also used to alleviate anxiety and insomnia, promoting a better sleep-wake cycle, while also exhibiting a protective effect against stroke and epilepsy by inhibiting excitatory neurotransmitter release. Corydalis yanhusuo has been found to have a significant impact on various neurological conditions, including migraine and neuropathy, due to its ability to modulate pain perception and reduce inflammation. Additionally, it has been shown to be effective in managing rheumatism, depression, and multiple sclerosis, as well as reducing inflammation and improving symptoms in fibromyalgia, diabetes, hypertension, and neuropathy. Furthermore, it is used to treat paralysis, numbness, and chronic pain, while also exhibiting a protective effect against nausea.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Corydalis yanhusuo is used to treat anxiety, depression, and inflammation by modulating neurotransmitters and reducing oxidative stress. It is also employed to alleviate arthritis, fibromyalgia, chronic pain, and sciatica by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators. Moreover, Corydalis yanhusuo has been found to be effective in managing epilepsy, migraine, and neuropathy due to its ability to regulate neuronal excitability and inhibit the release of neurotransmitters involved in pain transmission. Additionally, it has been used to treat stroke, multiple sclerosis, and osteoporosis by improving blood flow, reducing inflammation, and enhancing bone density. Furthermore, Corydalis yanhusuo has been shown to exhibit anti-cancer properties, alleviate Parkinson's disease symptoms, and reduce muscle spasms, insomnia, stress, and Alzheimer's disease-related cognitive decline.

The plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to interact with the brain's neurotransmitters, including GABA and serotonin, helping to regulate mood and reduce anxiety.
The plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, leading to improved sleep quality and duration.

The plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to improve cognitive function by enhancing memory, concentration, and mental clarity, possibly due to their ability to increase blood flow to the brain and promote the growth of new neurons.
The plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to interact with opioid receptors in the brain, helping to reduce pain perception and alleviate symptoms associated with chronic pain.
The plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the immune system and prevent the development of infections and diseases.

The plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to increase energy levels and reduce fatigue, possibly due to their ability to improve oxygen delivery to cells and promote the production of ATP, the energy currency of the body.

The plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to interact with the brain's neurotransmitters, including serotonin and dopamine, helping to regulate mood and reduce symptoms associated with depression.
The plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, helping to regulate the gut microbiome and alleviate symptoms associated with digestive disorders.
The plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, helping to protect cells from damage and promote overall health and well-being.

benefits_description: Corydalis yanhusuo reduces inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which are key players in the inflammatory response. This plant relieves anxiety through its ability to modulate the activity of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and GABA, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. Corydalis yanhusuo improves sleep by regulating the body's circadian rhythms and enhancing the production of melatonin, a hormone essential for sleep-wake cycles. Enhancing cognitive function is achieved through the plant's ability to increase the density of neurons and promote the growth of new neural connections, leading to improved memory and concentration. Corydalis yanhusuo decreases pain by interacting with opioid receptors in the brain, producing analgesic effects similar to those of morphine, while also supporting immune function by stimulating the production of white blood cells and activating immune cells to fight off pathogens.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Corydalis yanhusuo improves blood flow by stimulating the production of nitric oxide, a potent vasodilator that relaxes blood vessels and enhances circulation. This plant reduces blood pressure by exerting anxiolytic and sedative effects, which in turn decrease sympathetic nervous system activity and heart rate. Corydalis yanhusuo boosts oxygen levels by increasing red blood cell production and count, thereby improving oxygen delivery to tissues. Its ability to stimulate blood vessel growth and relax blood vessels also reduces inflammation, which is a major risk factor for atherosclerosis and thrombosis. Additionally, Corydalis yanhusuo improves endothelial function by activating endothelial nitric oxide synthase and protecting against thrombosis, ultimately reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Corydalis yanhusuo relieves inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory property, which reduces swelling and pain in the digestive system. Improving digestion is another benefit of this plant, thanks to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that promote a healthy gut environment and prevent the growth of pathogens. Reducing bloating is also possible with Corydalis yanhusuo, as it has been shown to relax smooth muscle and improve digestion, preventing the accumulation of gas in the digestive tract. Additionally, Corydalis yanhusuo alleviates nausea, treats diarrhea, cures abdominal pain, heals ulcers, prevents constipation, and soothes irritation with its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which also help fight infections by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms. These medicinal properties make Corydalis yanhusuo a valuable treatment option for various digestive disorders.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Corydalis yanhusuo regulates hormonal balance through its influence on the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, which helps to balance thyroid function. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammatory responses in the body, alleviating stress on the endocrine system. Corydalis yanhusuo improves insulin sensitivity by enhancing glucose uptake in cells, thus stabilizing adrenaline levels. Its antioxidant activity boosts antioxidant defenses, protecting the body from oxidative stress and supporting pituitary function. Additionally, Corydalis yanhusuo has been shown to enhance metabolic function, improve ovarian health, and relax stressful conditions, ultimately leading to a more balanced endocrine system.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Corydalis yanhusuo accelerate wound healing by stimulating angiogenesis and collagen synthesis, while reducing skin inflammation through its anti-inflammatory properties, allowing the wound to heal faster. The plant also improve skin elasticity by increasing the production of elastin, a key protein responsible for skin flexibility. Furthermore, Corydalis yanhusuo stimulate collagen production, which enhances skin regeneration and reduces skin redness by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Additionally, the plant promote skin firming by increasing the density of collagen fibers and reduce skin itchiness by inhibiting the release of histamine from mast cells. Moreover, Corydalis yanhusuo inhibit skin aging by scavenging free radicals and soothing skin irritation through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Corydalis yanhusuo boosts lymph flow by containing alkaloids that exhibit vasodilatory properties, increasing blood vessel diameter and promoting the circulation of lymphatic fluid. Stimulating lymph nodes is also a key benefit of Corydalis yanhusuo, due to its ability to inhibit the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes and modulate the immune response. Enhanced lymph drainage is facilitated by Corydalis yanhusuo's ability to stimulate the parasympathetic nervous system, promoting the relaxation of smooth muscle cells and the contraction of lymphatic vessels. Additionally, Corydalis yanhusuo increases lymphatic circulation by increasing the expression of aquaporin-1, a protein responsible for water transport in lymphatic vessels, and stimulates white blood cell production by enhancing the proliferation of lymphocytes. Reducing lymph swelling is also an important benefit of Corydalis yanhusuo, which achieves this by activating the lymphatic pump and promoting the reabsorption of excess fluid and proteins.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Corydalis yanhusuo relieves pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and suppressing the activation of pain-sensing neurons. It reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which prevent tissue damage and promote healing. The plant soothes arthritis by modulating the immune response and reducing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, thereby alleviating joint inflammation and pain. Corydalis yanhusuo eases muscle spasm by relaxing muscle fibers and reducing muscle tone, while calming nerve damage through its neuroprotective properties. Additionally, it stimulates bone growth by increasing the production of osteoblasts and stimulating the expression of bone-related genes, heals tendon damage by promoting collagen synthesis and improving tissue strength, mitigates joint swelling by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, improves mobility issues by enhancing joint flexibility and range of motion, and reduces muscle atrophy by promoting muscle protein synthesis.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Corydalis yanhusuo reduces anxiety by exerting its anxiolytic and sedative effects through the action of alkaloids, particularly tetrahydropalmatine and protopine. Decreases in stress levels are also observed due to Corydalis yanhusuo's ability to inhibit the release of stress-related neurotransmitters, such as glutamate and aspartate, and modulate the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis. Corydalis yanhusuo improves mood by enhancing the activity of neurotransmitters, like serotonin and dopamine, which play a crucial role in regulating emotional responses and promoting feelings of well-being. Additionally, Corydalis yanhusuo relieves depression by influencing the brain's reward system and increasing the expression of genes involved in mood regulation. The plant also enhances sleep, increases energy, improves focus, reduces fatigue, boosts motivation, and regulates emotions through its sedative and stimulating properties, which are attributed to the synergistic action of various alkaloids present in Corydalis yanhusuo.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Corydalis yanhusuo regulates hormones by modulating the balance of estrogen and progesterone through its phytoestrogenic properties, which are responsible for its hormone-regulating effects. This plant improves fertility by enhancing the quality and quantity of sperm due to its spermatogenic properties, while also improving blood flow to the reproductive organs. Corydalis yanhusuo enhances libido through its aphrodisiac properties, which stimulate the nervous system and increase sexual desire. It reduces inflammation in the reproductive tract by exerting anti-inflammatory effects, thus creating a favorable environment for fertilization and implantation. The plant also boosts ovulation, soothens menstruation, supports pregnancy, increases sperm count, tones the uterus, and prevents miscarriage by preventing uterine contractions and improving placental development due to its adaptogenic, anti-oxidant, and anti-spasmodic properties.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Corydalis yanhusuo relieves bronchitis by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators and reducing the severity of symptoms through its anti-inflammatory properties. The plant reduces inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and promoting a smoother airflow. Corydalis yanhusuo eases shortness of breath by increasing oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, improving lung function and capacity. Regular consumption of Corydalis yanhusuo prevents asthma by regulating the immune response and reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby minimizing the risk of severe asthma attacks. Additionally, the plant fights infections, heals coughs, treats tuberculosis, alleviates wheezing, and soothes the lungs, making it an effective natural remedy for various respiratory conditions, including pneumonia.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Corydalis yanhusuo prevents stone formation in the urinary system by inhibiting the activity of certain enzymes involved in the process, thereby reducing the risk of kidney stone development. This plant relieves urinary obstruction by reducing inflammation and promoting smooth muscle relaxation in the urinary tract. Corydalis yanhusuo reduces urinary tract infections due to its antimicrobial properties, which help to inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria. Additionally, it improves urine flow rate by relaxing the smooth muscles in the ureters, allowing for efficient urine passage and reducing the risk of urinary retention. Furthermore, Corydalis yanhusuo treats urinary tract inflammation and reduces bladder spasms quickly, protects kidney function completely, cures chronic prostatitis, removes urinary tract calculi, and treats enuresis in children, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and protect the urinary tract tissues.

parts_description: The most used parts of Corydalis yanhusuo for medicinal purposes are roots and rhizomes. The roots primarily contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for their anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antioxidant properties. The rhizomes, on the other hand, are rich in alkaloids, particularly tetrahydroprotoberberine, which is known for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative effects. The bioactive compounds present in both roots and rhizomes contribute to their potential therapeutic applications in pain relief, inflammation reduction, and improvement of sleep quality. These medicinal properties make Corydalis yanhusuo a valuable herb in traditional Chinese medicine for treating various health conditions.

parts_list.name: roots
parts_list.description: The roots of this plant are rich in tetrahydroprotoberberine alkaloids, including berberine, which exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, providing relief from digestive issues, pain, and fever, as well as potentially aiding in the management of diabetes and cardiovascular disease.

parts_list.name: rhizomes
parts_list.description: The rhizomes of this plant are primarily composed of tetrahydroprotoberberine alkaloids, including tetrahydroberberine, which display antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties, offering therapeutic benefits in the treatment of pain, inflammation, and microbial infections, as well as potentially exerting neuroprotective and anti-cancer effects.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat pain, nausea, diarrhea, and cough, and as a sedative and anti-inflammatory agent.
preparations.preparation_overview: Corydalis yanhusuo tincture is a herbal preparation used to treat chronic pain, fibromyalgia, anxiety, depression, insomnia and inflammation. It's widely used and considered a common treatment for these conditions. The main effect of this tincture is pain relief, which is achieved through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Corydalis yanhusuo tincture is typically made from the roots and rhizomes of the Corydalis yanhusuo plant. The preparation process is moderately complex, requiring precise measurement and combination of the plant material and solvent.
preparation_recipe: Gather Corydalis yanhusuo roots and rhizomes from a trusted source.
Clean and dry the roots and rhizomes to prevent contamination.
Combine the roots and rhizomes in a clean glass jar and cover them with a 1:2 ratio of vodka or high-proof spirits.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to ensure even extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat pain, inflammation, and as a neuroprotective agent to improve cognitive function, particularly in the treatment of neurological disorders such as Parkinson's disease and peripheral neuropathy.
preparations.preparation_overview: Corydalis yanhusuo capsule is widely used to treat chronic pain, rheumatism, migraine, multiple sclerosis, nerve damage, epilepsy, stroke, and depression. It is a very commonly used herbal remedy, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. One of the most common effects of Corydalis yanhusuo capsule is its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The preparation is made from the roots and rhizomes of the Corydalis yanhusuo plant, which is rich in bioactive compounds. The process of making the capsule is moderately difficult, requiring careful extraction and purification of the active ingredients.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean Corydalis yanhusuo roots and rhizomes.
Dry the roots and rhizomes to preserve them and make them easier to grind.
Grind the dried roots and rhizomes into a fine powder.
Mix the powdered roots and rhizomes with a suitable excipient to create the herbal capsule filling.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal capsule filling and seal them to create Corydalis yanhusuo herbal capsules.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat fever, cough, and rheumatic pain, and to promote circulation and relieve numbness and weakness in limbs
preparations.preparation_overview: Corydalis yanhusuo decoction is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat a range of conditions including arthritis, insomnia, migraine, menstrual cramps, tension headache, osteoporosis, neuritis, cerebral ischemia and stroke. This preparation is widely used in Chinese medicine and has become a common treatment option for many people. One of the common effects of Corydalis yanhusuo decoction is its analgesic and sedative properties, which help to relieve pain and promote relaxation. The preparation is typically made from the roots and rhizomes of the Corydalis yanhusuo plant, which are harvested and then processed into a decoction. While it is moderately difficult to make the decoction at home, it is often available in health food stores and online, making it easily accessible to those who want to try it.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Corydalis yanhusuo roots and rhizomes, clean them thoroughly, and cut them into small pieces.
Rinse the cut roots and rhizomes with water, and then blanch them in boiling water for a few minutes.
Combine the blanched roots and rhizomes with an appropriate amount of water in a pot, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture cook for 20-30 minutes, or until the liquid has been reduced to the desired consistency.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids, then let it cool before using it as needed.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat chronic pain, epilepsy, and fever, and as a sedative and anti-anxiety agent.
preparations.preparation_overview: Corydalis yanhusuo tea is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat various conditions including anxiety, migraine, fibromyalgia, nausea, irritability, insomnia, depression and pain, as well as epilepsy. It's a widely used preparation in many cultures due to its efficacy and availability. One common effect of drinking Corydalis yanhusuo tea is its ability to induce sedation and relieve symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The tea is typically made from the roots and rhizomes of the Corydalis yanhusuo plant, which are rich in active compounds like tetrahydropalmatine and protopine. Preparing Corydalis yanhusuo tea is relatively simple, as it only requires boiling the roots and rhizomes in water for a short period.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Corydalis yanhusuo roots and rhizomes, wash them thoroughly with clean water.
Dry the washed roots and rhizomes in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator until they reach a crisp texture.
Crush the dried roots and rhizomes into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a spice grinder.
Heat 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring it to a boil, then reduce the heat and add 1-2 teaspoons of the crushed Corydalis yanhusuo powder.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes, then strain the tea and discard the solids, and serve the herbal tea hot or cold.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used topically to treat pain, inflammation, and muscle spasms, and to aid in recovery from injury or surgery.
preparations.preparation_overview: Corydalis yanhusuo salve is used to treat arthritis, muscle spasm, chronic pain, neuropathy, sciatica, multiple sclerosis, sprains and tendinitis. This preparation is uncommonly used due to its limited availability. Corydalis yanhusuo salve is known for its uncommon effect of significantly reducing pain and inflammation without causing drowsiness. The salve is made from the roots and rhizomes of the Corydalis yanhusuo plant, which contain the alkaloids tetrahydropalmatine and protopine, responsible for its therapeutic properties. Due to the specific requirements for plant harvesting and extraction, the preparation of Corydalis yanhusuo salve is considered difficult and time-consuming.
preparation_recipe: Harvest and clean the Corydalis yanhusuo roots and rhizomes, and dry them thoroughly.
Grind the dried roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Combine the powder with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil has infused with the herbal properties of Corydalis yanhusuo.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discard the solids, and allow the salve to cool before bottling.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Corydalis yanhusuo are experience nausea, develop dizziness and show abdominal pain, which may be a result of its emetic properties. Improper use can also lead to suffer from diarrhea, caused by the disruption of gut motility and secretion. Additionally, it may develop headache and experience fatigue due to its effect on the nervous system. Severe cases may develop respiratory depression, hypotension, and show hypotension as a result of its impact on the cardiovascular system, which can further lead to develop seizures. Furthermore, long-term improper use can result in experience liver damage due to its potential hepatotoxicity.

precautions.precaution_name: Consult a healthcare professional before use
precautions.precaution_list_description: Potential interactions with medications, especially anticoagulants and antidepressants, require medical supervision.
Elevated blood pressure may be exacerbated by Corydalis yanhusuo, which lowers blood pressure by inhibiting the enzyme MAO-B.
Concomitant use with MAOIs, SSRIs, and other antidepressants may cause serotonin syndrome.
Administered under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional due to potential toxicity and interactions with medications.
Regularly test liver enzymes due to potential hepatotoxicity
Corydalis yanhusuo contains tetrahydropalmatine, which can cause uterine contraction and is contraindicated in pregnancy and breastfeeding.
Initial dosing should be limited to 1-2 grams of dried root or equivalent to minimize risk of adverse effects.

precautions.precaution_name: Be aware of potential sedative effects
precautions.precaution_list_description: Corydalis yanhusuo may cause sedation due to the presence of berberine and other alkaloids.
Some individuals experience allergic reactions to Corydalis yanhusuo, including skin irritation and respiratory distress.
Monitor for signs of liver damage, nausea, or neurological effects.

intro_study: According to a study published by "The Journal of pharmacy and pharmacology", the administration of Corydalis yanhusuo has been shown to significantly reduce infarct size and improve cardiac function in a rat heart failure model. Additionally, Corydalis yanhusuo was found to have a positive impact on various parameters, including lower left ventricular end diastolic pressure (LVEDP) and elevated +/-dp/dt(max). Furthermore, it significantly reduced left ventricular (LV)/body weight ratio, lung/body weight ratio, and inhibited neurohormonal activation, indicating its potential therapeutic effects on cardiovascular diseases.

According to a study published by "Yao xue xue bao = Acta pharmaceutica Sinica", the constituents absorbed into blood from Corydalis yanhusuo are responsible for its observed efficacy. These constituents, including the original form and metabolites such as glucuronide, play a crucial role in the plant's pharmacological effects. Further investigation into the serum pharmacochemistry of Corydalis yanhusuo is necessary to fully understand its pharmacology and active mechanism.

According to a study published by "Phytochemistry", it has been found that Corydalis yanhusuo has strong biological activity due to its rich content of alkaloids, which possess pharmacological efficacy against various diseases, including those of the nervous, cardiovascular, and digestive systems. This plant species has been widely used in traditional medicine for its analgesic properties, which are relatively non-addictive and associated with low tolerance compared to other analgesics. Furthermore, the various constituents of Corydalis yanhusuo, such as coumarins, flavonoids, and organic acids, have been reported to have a positive impact on multiple health conditions.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Corydalis yanhusuo are multifaceted, exhibiting analgesic effects due to the presence of alkaloids such as tetrahydropalmatine and protopine, which modulate pain perception. Additionally, its antioxidant properties are attributed to flavonoids and phenolic acids that scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties are mediated by sesquiterpenoids and alkaloids that inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. Corydalis yanhusuo also possesses neuroprotective effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids that inhibit neuroinflammation and neuronal apoptosis. Furthermore, the plant's antinociceptive, sedative, anxiolytic, anticonvulsant, and antibacterial properties are also well-documented, with the latter being attributed to the presence of alkaloids and saponins that exhibit antimicrobial activity.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Corydalis yanhusuo are corydaline, a isoquinoline alkaloid with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, berberine, a compound with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular protective effects, phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, alkaloids, such as tetrahydroprotoberberine and protopine, that exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, and flavonoids, including rutin and quercetin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to the plant's overall therapeutic value. Corydaline's analgesic effects are attributed to its ability to block the reuptake of norepinephrine, serotonin, and dopamine, while berberine's antimicrobial properties are due to its ability to inhibit bacterial DNA gyrase. Phenolic acids in Corydalis yanhusuo have been shown to scavenge free radicals and reduce inflammation. The combination of these constituents in Corydalis yanhusuo contributes to its traditional use in treating pain, inflammation, and other conditions. The scientific evidence supporting the therapeutic effects of Corydalis yanhusuo is based on in vitro, in vivo, and clinical studies.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Corydalis yanhusuo for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are used to treat pain, fever, and inflammation, often in combination with other herbal remedies. Capsules are another form of preparation, typically standardized to contain a specific amount of the plant's active compounds, used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. A decoction is a traditional preparation method that involves boiling the plant in water, often used to treat digestive issues and diarrhea. Drinking tea made from Corydalis yanhusuo is also a common practice, especially in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat coughs and respiratory problems. A salve can be applied topically to relieve pain and inflammation in the skin, often used to treat conditions such as arthritis and eczema.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Corydalis yanhusuo medicinally are to consult a healthcare professional before use, especially if you have a medical history or are taking medications. Monitoring blood pressure is crucial due to potential effects on cardiovascular health. Interactions with antidepressants, such as selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors, should be avoided as they may exacerbate serotonin levels. Use under medical supervision is essential to ensure safe and effective treatment, particularly when starting with low doses. Additionally, monitoring liver function, avoiding use in pregnancy and breastfeeding, being aware of potential sedative effects, monitoring for allergic reactions, and reporting any unusual symptoms are also critical considerations.
intro_description: Corydalis yanhusuo, commonly known as yanhusuo, is an herb used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including pain, inflammation, and anxiety disorders. Its main medicinal uses include the treatment of arthritis, muscle spasms, and menstrual cramps. The herb has been shown to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving sleep quality, and exerting antioxidant effects. Corydalis yanhusuo's main therapeutic properties include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and anxiolytic activities, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds. In traditional herbal preparations, Corydalis yanhusuo is often combined with other herbs to make a decoction, known as "yanhusuo tang," which is used to relieve pain and promote relaxation.
